license_requirements: Visitors to Australia may generally drive on a valid overseas license for the duration of a short-term visit, provided the license is current. If the license is not in English, an official translation or International Driving Permit (IDP) is recommended. A full license held for at least one year is required by most rental suppliers. Young driver surcharges and minimum age requirements vary by supplier. We recommend confirming specific requirements with your chosen supplier before booking.
meta_title: Car Rental in Australia — Sydney, Melbourne, Gold Coast & More
meta_description: Find car rental options across Australia. Explore pickup locations in Sydney, Melbourne, and the Gold Coast, plus major Australian airports.
meta_description_template: Find car rental options across {country_name}. Explore pickup locations in major cities and airports.
driving_notes: Traffic drives on the left in Australia. Speed limits are 50 km/h in urban areas unless otherwise posted, 100 km/h on rural roads, and up to 110 km/h on some freeways. Random breath testing is conducted regularly. Exercise caution when driving at dawn and dusk in rural areas due to wildlife on roads. If driving long distances in regional or remote areas, plan fuel stops carefully as distances between service stations can be significant.
toll_information: Electronic tolls operate on major motorways and tunnels in Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane. Most toll roads are cashless and require an electronic tag or registered account. Rental suppliers typically offer toll pass products or bill tolls post-rental. Confirm the toll arrangement with your supplier before departure.
fuel_notes: Fuel is sold in litres. Unleaded petrol, premium unleaded, diesel, and LPG are available in cities and major towns. In remote areas, fuel stops can be far apart. Electric vehicle charging is available in cities and along major highway corridors.
parking_notes: Parking in Australian cities is regulated by time limits and clearway zones. CBD parking is expensive in major cities. Clearway zones prohibit stopping during peak traffic hours. Always check local signage.
seasonal_notes: Peak rental demand is concentrated in the summer school holiday period (December to January) and Easter. Queensland destinations see demand from southern state visitors during winter months. Ski resorts in New South Wales and Victoria generate winter rental demand from June to September.
border_crossing_notes: Rental vehicles may generally travel between Australian states. Some suppliers restrict certain vehicle types from crossing into specific states or territories. Agricultural inspection checkpoints may operate at some state borders. Confirm inter-state travel permissions with your supplier before travel.
